series_description:
Any document that officially initiates, rescinds, or
amends general office procedures of the TWDB.

Any document that initiates, rescinds, or amends a
regulation, policy, or procedure that governs the TWDB
programs, services, or projects.

Calendars, appointment books or
programs, and scheduling or itinerary records, in any
media, purchased with state funds or maintained by staff
during business hours that document appointments,
itineraries and other activities of the agency officials or
employees.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the calendars, appointment, and itinerary records
of elected officials, executive staff, board or commission members,
division directors, and program heads require archival review. Contact
the State Archives when these records have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type purchased with personal funds, but used
by a state official or employee to document his or her work activities
may be a state record and subject to this retention period. See Open
Records Decision 635 issued in December 1995 by the Attorney General.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process of planning new
or redefined programs, services or projects of an agency
that are not included in or directly related to other
records series in this schedule. Includes Facility Needs
Records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments: ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Minutes or notes taken and supporting documentation
used at an internal agency staff meeting.

series_title:
Rules, Policies, and Procedures –
Final and Working Files
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar
records distributed internally for the use of employees or
externally to the public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the rules, policies,
and procedures that govern the agency's programs,
services or projects and working files used in their
development.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Print masters of original version and all subsequent
revisions to an agency form, including any associated
design or design modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files containing organized
and consistent sets of complete and accurate electronic
records. Examples include, but are not limited to, data
tables of relational databases used by applications or
computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of AC to agency automated processing
files that the files do not fall under records series listed elsewhere.

series_description:
Any internally distributed manual, guidelines, or similar
records that establish standard office procedures for the
agency; for example, agency style manuals, telephone
protocols, mail room procedures, print shop and
photocopy ordering instructions.
comments: Includes out of office notifications, mail distribution, etc.

series_description:
Any record created by an agency to track any type of
incoming and outgoing correspondence or packages by
the U.S. Postal Service or by private couriers.
comments: Also known as a mail log.

series_description:
Logs, registers, or similar records documenting visitors.
Includes internal and external training registration logs.

This series documents state and federal grant projects
participated in or administered by state agencies. It
includes grant authorization records, which provide
evidence of the award of grants to or by agencies;
grant/project financial or performance reports, which are
periodic reports of financial activity and/or program
performance related to grants received or made by
agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Retention requirements may vary depending on the specific
federal funding agency. Records in this series may be subject to
Government Code 441.1855. Agencies must ensure that records are
retained for the appropriate retention period.

series_description:
Non-awarded, denied, or unfunded grant applications
and proposals.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers, presentations, or
reports delivered in conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches, papers, and presentations given by or
on behalf of executive staff, board or commission members, division
directors and program heads require archival review.

series_description:
Safety Data Sheets (SDS) that list information relating to
occupational safety and health for the use of various
substances and products.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: If Workplace Chemical Lists are not maintained, these records
must be maintained for 30 years.

series_description:
News, press releases, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency. Includes print,
electronic, audio, and audiovisual records. Also includes
files for conference outreach.

Logos for various TWDB-associated
programs.

Includes creative services and graphic design-related
elements, artwork, and final files for print and digital
marketing materials, certificates, agency exhibit
materials, template, promotional items and apparel,
state water and flood plans, signage, reports, photos,
graphics, and other agency branding.

series_title:
Bond Counsel (Contracts, Bond
Sales and Related Information)
series_description:
Services performed for TWDB relating to bond activity.
Bond Counsel firms provide opinions on the issuance of
tax-exempt and taxable bonds. These documents relate
to the contracts themselves and their payments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Forms and logs used to reconcile batches submitted for
processing against batches received and processed
(includes document logs).

Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all
voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including
garnishment or other court ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Payroll records and registers documenting employee
earnings, wages, and pay. This may include but is not
limited to payroll input records, summary statements,
payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and payroll
history.
comments: Electronic records

series_description:
Payroll: FICA records (W2s); Tax Withholding (W9’s); IRS -
Quarterly, 941, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
Federal requirement is 3 years from the date of the submission of the
final Financial Status Report. Files must be reviewed by staff before final
disposition.

series_description:
Records documenting the amount of pension or deferred
compensation earned by individual employees.

Reports providing statistical information on labor force
submitted to Texas Workforce Commission (TWC), State
Auditor's Office (SAO), etc.

series_title:
Reimbursable Activities, Requests
and Authorizations to Engage in
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel; participation in
educational programs, workshops, or college classes; or
for other work-related activities for which the expenses
of the employee are defrayed or reimbursed.

Claims; invoices; statements; copies of checks and
purchase orders; expenditure authorizations; ledgers;
encumbrance, purchase, general journal, expenditure,
and special vouchers; and similar records that serve to
document disbursements, including those documenting
claims for and reimbursement to employees for travel
and other expenses.
comments:
Federal requirement is 3 years from the date of the submission of the
final Financial Status Report. Files must be reviewed by staff before final
disposition.

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing registers, account
cards, deposit warrants, cash receipts, credit card
receipts, receipts books, cash transfers, daily cash
reports, reconciliations, general journal vouchers, special
vouchers, and similar records that serve to document
money owed to or received by TWDB and its collection or
receipt.

Required by the General Appropriations
Act. Includes encumbrances and documentation about
budget transfers and revisions, as well as detail charts of
accounts.

Periodic reports compiled from information entered into
the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Yearly report compiled from information entered into the
Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).

series_title:
TWRFA - Texas Water Resource
Finance Authority
series_description:
Legal and executed document pertaining to respective
bonds issued by a borrower and held by the TWDB.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Signature cards or similar records establishing authority
of an agency employee to initiate or authorize financial
transactions on behalf of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Bearer Bonds

series_title:
Annual Arbitrage Rebate Liability
Calculation Report
series_description:
Arbitrage calculation documents related to IRS
compliance. Bonds: Arbitrage Reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_title:
Transcripts of Legal Proceedings,
Bond Transcript, Ordinance,
Resolution, Agreements.
series_description:
Legal and executed document pertaining to respective
bonds issued by a borrower and held by the TWDB, or
Loan Agreements or Master Agreements with funds owed
to the TWDB.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments: Records maintained in File Room 630.

series_description:
Bank statements, credit card statements, cancelled
checks, check registers, deposit slips, debit and credit
notices, reconciliations, and other banking related
records.

W-9 IRS Form used to request a taxpayer identification
number (TIN) for reporting information to the Internal
Revenue Service (IRS). This includes W-9 forms received
by a state agency from vendors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Includes bid requisitions & authorizations, invitations to
bid or propose, bid specifications, accepted and rejected
bids, bid tabulation & evaluations. a) Associated with a
contract executed, renewed, or amended on or after
September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes bid requisitions & authorizations, invitations to
bid or propose, bid specifications, accepted and rejected
bids, bid tabulation & evaluations. b) Associated with a
contract executed, renewed, or amended on or before
August 31, 2015.

Includes bid requisitions & authorizations, invitations to
bid or propose, bid specifications, accepted and rejected
bids, bid tabulation & evaluations. c) Unsuccessful bids
that do not meet agency submission requirements and
are not included in bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdrawn, missed submission deadline, incomplete
submission, etc.).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Log, register, etc., providing a record of purchase orders
issued, orders received, and similar data on procurement
status.

Including any supporting documentation created and/or
used to justify and support legislative appropriations
requests by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of Legislative Appropriation Requests
must be submitted to the Texas State Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A). Working files and related documentation used
in creating the final plan are not subject to archival review and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Draft legislation, fiscal notes, bill analyses and related
correspondence.

Sunset Review Report, agency self-study, and other
correspondence and supporting documentation related
to the Sunset review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related documentation includes the agency response
to the commission's draft report, and documentation of the agency's
implementation of the commission's final recommendations. The final
version of the agency self-evaluation report must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(1).

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor and legislature
as required by an agency's enabling statutes, including
annual narrative reports if required by statute. Includes
working files and related documentation used in creating
final report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3. The requirement is met by sending the required copies of the
reports to the Texas State Publications Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission. Working files and related
documentation used in creating the final report may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Information resources and operational strategic plans
prepared in accordance with 2054.095 and 2056.002,
Government Code.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of Strategic Plans must be submitted
to the Texas State Publications Depository Program per 13 TAC
3.3(a)(2)(C). Working files and related documentation used in creating
the final plan are not subject to archival review and may be disposed of
at the expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure of an agency
and the relationships and relative ranks of its
departments and job positions.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts showing the overall arrangement and
administrative structure of the state agency need to be transferred to
the Archives and Information Services Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission. Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are not considered archival.

series_description:
Includes indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers, guides,
etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry the same retention period and
archival code of the records they support.

series_description:
Self-evaluation and plans documenting compliance with
the requirements of the Americans with Disabilities Act.
Includes Accessibility Coordination Group.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference,
and similar documents whose submission by candidates
for vacant positions is required on the application form,
by application procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to the counseling
of an employee for work- related, personal, or substance
abuse problems. Usually maintained at the supervisory
level except in those agencies with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or advertisements of
job openings, promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

Job evaluations, performance appraisals, or other similar
documents used to evaluate the performance of an
employee.

Disciplinary actions are those actions, which may affect
pay, status, or tenure. They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the best interests
of the state, the agency, or the employee work force, and
for failure to improve performance or conduct following
imposition of corrective action. May include cause for
demotion, suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for
failure to give written notice of resignation. May also be
used to document evidence of employee self-
improvement efforts, as well as favorable and
unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or change
information concerning the records of an employee
including pay grade, position classification, employee
number, evaluation date, and termination of
employment. Includes Income Adjustment Authorizations
used to make increases or decreases to an employees'
gross pay, FICA, retirement, or in the computation of
taxes.

Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill
statements, for positions in an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job applicants or
agency employees obtained from the Department of
Public Safety (DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is authorized to obtain a criminal history
record information from DPS must refer to its agency's legislation or
see Subchapter F, Chapter 411, Government Code for appropriate
retention and use of this information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, transcripts, test scores, or
similar records documenting the training, testing, or
continuing education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Employment Eligibility,
Documentation or Verification of
series_description: Federal reporting form (INS I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation requires that INS I-9 forms be retained for
3 years from date of hire or 1 year after separation of the employee,
whichever later. Agencies should make certain that the INS I-9 forms for
employees who terminate from an agency less than 3 years from date
of hire are kept for the 3-year retention period.

series_description: Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for both regular employees and
apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for contractors and
subcontractors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Employee benefit plans such as pension; life, health, and
disability insurance; deferred compensation; etc.,
including amendments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Reports providing statistical information on labor force
including Annual Statewide Hiring Practice Reports, EEO
Workforce Availability Reports, Military Workforce
Reports, reports to the State Auditor's Office, etc.

Records relating to review and monitoring of job
classifications within an agency.

Reports to the agency from TWC or its predecessor
pertaining to employees.

Any internally distributed manuals, guidelines, or similar
records that define agency wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of an agency.

Any documents detailing the procedural duties and
responsibilities of agency positions on a position-by-
position basis.

Any reports compiled by an agency on aspects of
personnel staffing, including listings of all staff by
program or name, staff hired during a month, detailed
listings of employees within its organizational structure,
position vacancies, analyses of turnover rates and
seasonality of employment, etc.

Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and checklists
required of job applicants or of current personnel to
qualify for promotion or transfer, including validation
records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each different test (different in terms of either
questions or administration procedures) should be retained for the
period indicated.

series_description:
Completed aptitude, competency, or skills test papers of
job applicants or of current personnel taking a test to
qualify for promotion or transfer.
Includes documentation used to complete EEO reports.

Reports, studies, aggregated or summary data, and
similar documentation compiled to monitor and
demonstrate compliance with the federal Equal Pay Act.

series_title:
Occupational Accident Reports and
Associated Documentation
series_description:
Accident or occupational disease reports (by supervisors
and employees) and other associated reports required to
be submitted to the Texas Department of Insurance or its
predecessors or maintained internally on accident
frequency.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title: Time Off and/or Sick Leave Request
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for vacation, compensatory,
sick, Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool
leave, and other types of authorized leave, and
supporting documentation.

Requests submitted, approvals, number of hours
transferred in and out, emergency leave, military leave,
FMLA, extended sick leave

"Employee’s Withholding Exemption Certificate"
maintained by the TWDB.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Forms used to deposit employee’s earnings into a
specified personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records documenting individual employee’s hours
worked, including work schedules and documentation
evidencing adherence to or deviation from normal hours
for those employees working on fixed schedules.

Records documenting number of hours transferred in and
out of comprehensive sick leave pool.

Agreements between employee and agency authorizing
certain actions, including acknowledgement forms,
telecommuting agreements, outside/secondary
employment authorizations, or other documentation that
show proof of receipt and awareness of and adherence to
agency policies and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement regarding the terms and
conditions of employment of an individual and a contract
with a vendor for temporary staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or after September 1,
2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement regarding the terms and
conditions of employment of an individual and a contract
with a vendor for temporary staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31,
2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments: NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for retention period context.

series_description:
Personnel records maintained on each intern. May
include, but is not limited to, applications, eligibility
verification forms, evaluations, timesheets, training and
educational achievement records, and related
information.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Internship applications, resumes, outside employment
forms, background authorization releases, and selection
records for applicants who are not selected.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Employee Drug Testing and
Screening Records – Positive Results
and Calibration
series_description:
Records of employee alcohol test results indicating an
alcohol concentration of 0.02 or greater; records of
employee verified positive controlled substances test
results; documentation of refusals to take required
alcohol and/or controlled substances tests; employee
evaluation and referrals; calibration documentation;
records related to the administration of the alcohol and
controlled substances testing programs; copy of each
annual calendar year summary.
legal_citation:
49 CFR 382.403 for
commercial motor vehicle
drivers.

series_title:
Employee Drug Testing and
Screening Records – Negative
Results
series_description:
Records of negative and canceled controlled substances
test results and alcohol test results with a concentration
of less than 0.02.

Employee or applicant requests for reasonable
accommodation under the ADA (Americans with
Disabilities Act).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Polygraph examinations and results that are administered
as a part of the employment selection process.

Records relating to the issuance of keys, identification
cards, building passes, passwords, signed statements or
similar instruments of access to agency or state facilities,
equipment, or automated systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Automated software applications and operating system
files including job control language, program
listing/source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for access to electronic records must be
retained for the period of time required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and maintenance
requirements of computer hardware such as operating
manuals, hardware / operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, and equipment control systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for access to electronic records must be
retained for the period of time required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical characteristics
necessary for reading or processing of electronic records
and their timely, authorized disposition including
documentation describing how a system operates and
necessary for using the system such as user guides,
system or sub- system definitions, system specifications,
input and output specifications, and system flow charts,
program descriptions and documentation such as
program flowcharts, program maintenance logs, change
notices, and other records that document modifications
to computer programs, and data documentation
necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate, and interpret
data in an automated system such as a data element
dictionary, file layout, code book or table, and other
records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure,
logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the time period required to access the
records.

series_description:
Reports summarizing efficiency of facility space
utilization, which may include data on room usage,
demand, allocation, and capacity.

Includes mileage, fuel/oil consumption, passengers
carried and other related operational information.
comments: Provided to the Comptroller and Office of Fleet Management

series_description:
Includes records documenting lease agreements entered
into by TWDB.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Documentation relating to the capital and fixed assets of
a state agency, including equipment or property history
cards containing data on initial cost, depreciation
schedules or summaries used for capital outlay
budgeting, and property sale, auction, or disposal records
of agency owned equipment and property.

Includes all correspondence and documentation relating
to requests for records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments: Electronic records

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and documentation relating
to requests for records that are excepted under the
Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code),
including records provided to the legislature under
section 552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: Electronic records

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency, and
adopted rules, open meetings notices; or any other
documents required by law to be submitted to the Texas
Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments: Electronic

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of the Texas Water
Development Board in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit. Includes EEOC files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library and Archives Commission for archival
preservation. See page ix of the Texas State Records Retention Schedule
for more information.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of the Attorney General on an
agency’s Public Information activities.

Agency copy. A records retention schedule (i.e., form SLR
105) that identifies the records that are created and
maintained by an agency, provides the minimum
timeframes the records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This series may include
working files and documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and Archives
Commission.
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of records to/from
onsite and offsite storage areas or a transfer of physical
custody.

Logs or similar records listing records destroyed or
transferred to the Archives and Information Services
Division, Texas State Library and Archives Commission,
showing records series title, dates of records, and date
destroyed or transferred.
comments: CAUTION: Disposition can mean destroyed or transferred.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to capture records
inventory information, including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of a records
series.

Records management plans and similar records that
establish the policies and procedures under which
records and information are managed in an agency.

Financial assistance documents associated with state
water plan consistency and federal population projection
review requirements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
(A) Senate Bill 1 Regional Water Planning Groups
Notebook Files; (B) Senate Bill 1 ICB Files.
comments: Retained at Agency in office of Director of Water Supply Planning.

series_title:
Public Agency Inquiries and
Technical Assistance Requests -
State Water Plan (SWP) Database
series_description:
All Water Use, Projections, and Planning (WUPP) data
requests.
comments: Electronic records

series_description:
Public Comments on Senate Bill 1 Water, and Planning
Region Delineation, 1997-1998 - implementation of SB1.
comments: Retained at agency.

series_description:
A comprehensive document per TWC Sec. 16.051 that
projects long-term water demands for all regions of the
state and proposes water supply solutions to meet those
demands.

series_title:
Texas State Water Plan - Supporting
Documentation
series_description:
Supporting Documentation for the Texas State Water
Plan.
comments: In storage at SRC.

series_description:
Adopted population and water demand projections
utilized in state water plans.
comments: Electronic records

series_title:
Survey of Ground & Surface Water
Use
series_description:
Annual water use surveys of public water systems and
large
industrial/manufacturing facilities.
comments: Electronic storage only on agency server – scan and destroy paper.

series_description:
Includes program planning and adopted documents for
regional water plans per TWC Sec. 16.053.
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications and published technical papers must
be submitted to the Texas State Publications Depository Program, Texas
State Library and Archives Commission, by law (Government Code
441.101- 441.106). The Texas State Library and Archives Commission will
retain a copy of all state publications submitted to it on a continuing
basis, subject to periodic evaluation to determine if the publication
merits further retention. For additional information and requirements
concerning state publications made available to the public through the
Internet, consult the administrative rules of the Texas State Publications
Depository Program of the Texas State Library and Archives Commission
(13 TAC 3.1-3.16). Electronic records.

series_description:
Historic maps of natural features in Texas including
water, geologic features, wetlands, and topographic
information. Aerial and satellite photo prints (~1 million
frames) dating back to the 1930s to the 1990s over Texas.
Aerial photo mosaics and indexes accompany most of the
collections that are sourced from a variety of government
agencies including TxDOT, USGS, NRCS and Army Corp of
Engineers.
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry the same retention period and
archival code of the records they support.
ARCHIVES NOTE: The portion of this series that are Maps-Topographic
Historical Collection require archival review.
Paper Format.

series_description:
Dam data, elevation-storage relationship curves, and
daily elevation or storage data. Includes historical
documents, maps, photos, and misc. information relating
to reservoirs.

Electronic: well locations plotted on 7.5 topographic maps disposed.
Linen maps of well locations on county highway maps were scanned by
TCEQ in 2020 and saved on TWDB network drives. The physical linen
maps are currently stored in TSLAC Archives.

series_description:
Water availability determinations and allocations for the
1990 and 1997 state water plans
comments:
Files are stored electronically on the FEMA web-based database. FEMA
requires records to be available for three years after the closeout of the
grant date, or in this case, the year the CAV was performed. Additional,
supporting documents (e.g.: flood permit files, maps, variance files,
elevation certificates, correspondence, etc.) are kept on file for future
CAV reference. According to FEMA guidance, Community Assistance
Visit documents should be kept for at least the duration of any
identified deficiencies or violations, and ideally until they are fully
addressed, which could mean several years depending on the issue, to
monitor a community's progress in resolving floodplain management
issues; documentation should be kept to support any potential
enforcement actions if necessary.

series_description:
Includes reports, manuscripts and correspondence for
desalination, water reuse, rainwater harvesting, aquifer
storage recovery, brackish resource aquifer
characterization system, groundwater availability, water
quality records and other data that results in a published
report.
comments:
All files are stored electronically on the FEMA web-based database;
Community Information System, as well as tracked at the TWDB and
kept on the public drive.
Although communities are required to keep flood program records in
perpetuity, CAC records at the state level can be kept for 5 years to
document programmatic historical data.

series_title:
Groundwater - Located Well Data
by County
series_description:
Lower Rio Grande Valley; Parts of Loving, Pecos, Reeves,
Ward, and Winkler Counties in TX; Part of North Central
Texas; Part of Central Texas; Orange and Eastern
Jefferson Counties; Fort Bend County; Parts of the Rolling
Prairies Region of North Central Texas; El Paso County;
Bell, Burnet, Travis, Williamson and parts of adjacent
counties; In the vicinity of the cities of Henderson,
Jacksonville, Kilgore, Lufkin, Nacogdoches, Rusk, and
Tyler in East Texas; In and adjacent to Dripping Springs,
TX.; Southern High Plains of TX Includes un-plotted
drillers logs and well schedules which have been given a
unique number for the study on: High Plains, TX; The
Bone Springs –Victoria Peak Aquifer in the Dell Valley
area of TX; Southern High Plains of TX; Edwards Aquifer in
the Austin area; Terlingua area of TX; Carrizo-Wilcox
Aquifer in the Central Region of TX.

series_description:

Hard copies of data from the state’s historic (now

locations and rivers in the state. Data may be used for
scientific studies.

Water Quality (salinity, temperature, dissolved oxygen,
depth), Coastal Hydrology (gaged, modeled, diversions,
returns) , Water Circulation (velocity and direction),
Bathymetry, Model Input (tides, salinity, precipitation,
evaporation) and Modeled Output (water velocity and
direction, surface elevation, salinity), GPS- RTK Survey
Data.

Station IDs, names, locations (County, coordinates) for
the 46 evaporation stations in Texas.
